title: Strapatsada

overview.description: A simple Greek breakfast of softly scrambled eggs gently cooked with ripe tomatoes, olive oil and feta.

ingredients: 4 large ripe tomatoes, grated or finely chopped
1 tbsp chopped fresh oregano or parsley
method: Heat the olive oil over a medium heat.
Cook gently for 10–15 minutes until much of the liquid has evaporated.
Season lightly with black pepper. Add very little salt as the feta is already salty.
Pour the eggs into the tomatoes.
Stir slowly until the eggs are softly scrambled and still creamy.
Fold through most of the feta.
Scatter over the remaining feta and herbs.

learning.did-you-know: Strapatsada is a traditional Greek farmhouse breakfast that makes the most of summer tomatoes when they're at their sweetest.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How slowly reducing fresh tomatoes concentrates their sweetness before gently scrambling eggs into the sauce.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A wonderfully simple breakfast that makes the most of good tomatoes and teaches that a few quality ingredients are often all you need.
chapman-tips: The tomatoes should become thick and jammy before adding the eggs.
Cook the eggs gently—they should stay soft and creamy.
Taste before adding salt because feta contributes plenty.
Use the ripest tomatoes you can find.
